item.title: Topolski's Chronicle, V.5 No.7-8, 1957
item.creator: Topolski, Feliks, 1907-1989
item.description: Topolski's Chronicle is a series of broadsheets created by Feliks Topolski and distributed bimonthly to subscribers from 1953 through 1982. Topolski was a superlative draughtsman and covered current events with an artistic, unique style.

pages.typeset_text:
TOPOLSKI'S CHRONICLE No.7-8 (31-32) Vol.V 1957

nothing oftentimes and therefore never byten. - Sir Philip Sydney (1580)

866

U.S.A. STEEL

The drawings in this issue were made possible through an assignment by FORTUNE MAGAZINE. Drawing No.866 first appeared in FORTUNE (January 1957) within a portfolio "Topolski on Steel"

THIS DOUBLE ISSUE COSTS 7s. ($1.00). "TOPOLSKI'S CHRONICLE" IS PUBLISHED 24 TIMES A YEAR AT 3s.6d. (50 cents) A COPY. YEARLY SUBSCRIPTION RATES (WITH FOLDER AND INDEX) TO ANY PART OF THE WORLD ARE 14s. 6d. ($3.02 or equivalent): SHORTER PERIODS PRO RATA. AIR MAIL BY QUOTATION. SUBSCRIPTIONS FROM OUTSIDE THE STERLING AREA MAY BE PAID THROUGH THE UNESCO CO-OP SCHEME

Lithographed in Great Britain by CAPS Ltd., Dean Street, London W1, and published by Topolski's Chronicles Ltd., from Bridge Arch 158, Sutton Walk, London SE1

UNIVERSITY OF UTAH LIBRARIES
sketch_descriptions: Abstract sketch of industrial workers operating large machinery in a steel factory environment, showing energetic lines suggesting movement and mechanical activity.
A stylized figure of a seated person, drawn with minimal lines, appears above the factory scene, possibly symbolic or artistic representation.
pages.page_summary: This page from Topolski's Chronicle (1957) features a dynamic charcoal sketch capturing steel factory workers under the title "U.S.A. STEEL," originally published in Fortune magazine, accompanied by a quote from Sir Philip Sydney (1580).

pages.handwritten_text:
A Blast Furnace
Workers waiting for the "cast"
sketch_descriptions: On the left page, a charcoal sketch of several industrial workers wearing protective helmets and gear, seated and waiting, beneath a large curved pipe structure representing a blast furnace.
On the right page, a high contrast silhouette drawing of a single worker using a long-handled tool to stoke or tap the furnace, emphasized against a dark background.
pages.page_summary: This page features charcoal and silhouette sketches depicting industrial laborers near a blast furnace, capturing workers awaiting the furnace cast and a worker actively engaged with the furnace.

pages.handwritten_text: U.S.A. Steel near Pittsburgh, PA K. Eichie [?] Mellany [?] [illegible]
sketch_descriptions: A fiery industrial scene dominated by dark sketch lines and red-orange coloration possibly depicting a steel mill in operation with glowing molten metal or fire.
Three rough human figures in the foreground, two appearing to be workers and one standing with arms crossed.
pages.page_summary: An expressive sketch illustrating an industrial steel mill near Pittsburgh, capturing the intense heat and activity of the site along with workers in the foreground.

pages.handwritten_text:
In the 'project' of the "Merchant Mills" - from the operator controls the main electrical generator of the mill
Telefuntruck Stack Trailer; team on the Ohio River near Pittsburgh
sketch_descriptions: Two men operating or working near a control board with electrical generators or switches inside an industrial interior space.
A group of figures near a stack trailer with loose industrial scenery, identified as a team on the Ohio River near Pittsburgh.
pages.page_summary: The page shows sketches of industrial workers managing electrical equipment in a mill and a group around a stack trailer near the Ohio River close to Pittsburgh, with handwritten notes describing these scenes.
